Japan has over 6,800 islands.
The Japanese railway system is known for its punctuality.
Mount Fuji is an active volcano and Japan's highest peak.
Japan has more than 1,500 earthquakes every year.
The Japanese language has three writing systems: hiragana, katakana, and kanji.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Japan has the world's oldest company, Kongō Gumi, founded in 578 AD.
Cherry blossoms (sakura) are a symbol of spring and renewal in Japan.
Japan is home to the world's busiest pedestrian crossing in Shibuya.
The Japanese tea ceremony is a traditional ritual influenced by Zen Buddhism.
Japan has over 100 active volcanoes.
The Shinkansen (bullet train) can reach speeds of up to 320 km/h.
Japan is the world's largest consumer of fish.
The Japanese flag is called 'Nisshōki' or 'Hinomaru'.
Japan has a literacy rate of nearly 100%.
The Japanese celebrate coming of age at 20 years old.
Japan has four distinct seasons.
The Japanese bow as a form of greeting and respect.
Japan is known for its high-tech toilets with various features.
The Japanese currency is the yen.
Japan has a culture of removing shoes before entering homes.
The Japanese practice 'hanami', viewing cherry blossoms.
Japan is famous for its anime and manga culture.
The Japanese celebrate many festivals (matsuri) throughout the year.
Japan has a unique onsen (hot spring) bathing culture.
The Japanese New Year (Shōgatsu) is the most important holiday.
Japan is known for its meticulous attention to detail and craftsmanship.
The Japanese practice 'omotenashi', a culture of hospitality.
Japan has a low crime rate compared to many other countries.
The Japanese diet is considered one of the healthiest in the world.
'Amanatto' are sweetened azuki beans often served with tea as a small snack.
'Mono no aware' (物の哀れ) is an aesthetic of gentle sadness at the impermanence of things.
